# Jonathan Huang

> Jonathan Huang (Jon) is a physician-scientist in training — a fourth-year MD/PhD student in the Medical Scientist Training Program (MSTP) at Northwestern University Feinberg School of Medicine. He received his PhD in Biomedical Engineering from Northwestern in 2025, supervised by Dr. Mozziyar Etemadi. He graduated from Brown University in 2020 with degrees in Computer Science and Neuroscience. He was named to the 2026 Forbes 30 Under 30 list in Healthcare for his work developing generative AI tools for radiology.

His research focuses on developing, integrating, and evaluating custom-built generative AI models that improve real clinical workflows. These models support medical imaging interpretation — including chest X-rays and CT scans — as part of clinical care across the Northwestern Medicine health system. His 2025 paper in JAMA Network Open was the first to demonstrate clinical benefits of generative AI tools in radiology, showing up to 40% improvements in radiologist productivity.
